item.title: Nottingham triple killer assaulted husband and wife weeks before attacks, inquiry told
item.description: Valdo Calocane attacked two colleagues weeks before he killed three people in Nottingham.

item.title: HMS Dragon sets sail from Portsmouth and heads to Cyprus
item.description: The UK has deployed the Type 45 Destroyer to the Mediterranean region, after an Iranian drone struck the runway at the RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus on 1 March.

item.title: FOUR IRANIAN DIPLOMATS KILLED IN LEBANON ON SUNDAY IN ISRAELI ATTACK - NOURNEWS CITING DIPLOMAT
item.description: FOUR IRANIAN DIPLOMATS KILLED IN LEBANON ON SUNDAY IN ISRAELI ATTACK - NOURNEWS CITING DIPLOMAT

item.title:
HORMUZ CLOSURE CRIPPLES FOOD SECURITY
UN Trade and Development warns the Strait of Hormuz disruption—traffic down 97%—is spiking energy, fertilizer, transport, and insurance costs, echoing Covid-19 and Ukraine war shocks.
With 20% of global oil/LNG and one-third of seaborne fertilizer (16 million tonnes in 2024) routed through the strait, food prices surge hardest on vulnerable developing nations facing debt burdens and soaring borrowing costs. Poorest countries like Sudan, Somalia, and others face acute fertilizer shortages.
item.description:
HORMUZ CLOSURE CRIPPLES FOOD SECURITY

UN Trade and Development warns the Strait of Hormuz disruption—traffic down 97%—is spiking energy, fertilizer, transport, and insurance costs, echoing Covid-19 and Ukraine war shocks.

With 20% of global oil/LNG and one-third of seaborne fertilizer (16 million tonnes in 2024) routed through the strait, food prices surge hardest on vulnerable developing nations facing debt burdens and soaring borrowing costs. Poorest countries like Sudan, Somalia, and others face acute fertilizer shortages.

item.title:
ISRAEL TO BOOST DEFENSE BUDGET $13B FOR IRAN CONFLICT
Israel plans to increase its defense spending by ~40 billion shekels ($13B), about 2% of GDP, to fund the war with Iran, according to a finance official. The supplement is part of the 2026 revised budget, set for cabinet debate and possible approval Tuesday under PM Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.description:
ISRAEL TO BOOST DEFENSE BUDGET $13B FOR IRAN CONFLICT

Israel plans to increase its defense spending by ~40 billion shekels ($13B), about 2% of GDP, to fund the war with Iran, according to a finance official. The supplement is part of the 2026 revised budget, set for cabinet debate and possible approval Tuesday under PM Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title:
BRENT OIL TO STAY HIGH SHORT-TERM, SLIDE BELOW $80 BY YEAR-END – EIA
🔸Brent oil likely above $95/bbl over the next 2 months due to Middle East tensions; expected to drop to $70/bbl by end-2026.
🔸U.S. oil production projected at 13.6 mln bpd in 2026, rising to 13.8 mln bpd in 2027.
🔸Strait of Hormuz disruption will cut Middle East output temporarily; gradual recovery expected.
🔸U.S. gasoline prices forecast at $3.34/gal in 2026, $3.18/gal in 2027.
🔸OPEC+ unlikely to boost production significantly next year; global supply to exceed demand once Hormuz flows resume.
🔸Global inventories set to grow by 1.9 mln bpd in 2026 and 3.0 mln bpd in 2027.
item.description:
BRENT OIL TO STAY HIGH SHORT-TERM, SLIDE BELOW $80 BY YEAR-END – EIA

🔸Brent oil likely above $95/bbl over the next 2 months due to Middle East tensions; expected to drop to $70/bbl by end-2026.

🔸U.S. oil production projected at 13.6 mln bpd in 2026, rising to 13.8 mln bpd in 2027.

🔸Strait of Hormuz disruption will cut Middle East output temporarily; gradual recovery expected.

🔸U.S. gasoline prices forecast at $3.34/gal in 2026, $3.18/gal in 2027.

🔸OPEC+ unlikely to boost production significantly next year; global supply to exceed demand once Hormuz flows resume.

🔸Global inventories set to grow by 1.9 mln bpd in 2026 and 3.0 mln bpd in 2027.

item.title:
RAND PAUL WARNS HIGH OIL COULD HURT GOP IN MIDTERMS
Rand Paul warned Republicans could face a “disastrous” midterm election if oil prices stay high amid the conflict with Iran.
The senator criticized the rationale for U.S. strikes and said prolonged military action pushing oil above $100—and raising gasoline prices—could create major political headwinds for the Republican Party.
item.description:
RAND PAUL WARNS HIGH OIL COULD HURT GOP IN MIDTERMS

Rand Paul warned Republicans could face a “disastrous” midterm election if oil prices stay high amid the conflict with Iran.

The senator criticized the rationale for U.S. strikes and said prolonged military action pushing oil above $100—and raising gasoline prices—could create major political headwinds for the Republican Party.

item.title: WITKOFF: RUSSIANS SAID THEY HAVE NOT BEEN SHARING INTEL W/ IRAN
item.description: WITKOFF: RUSSIANS SAID THEY HAVE NOT BEEN SHARING INTEL W/ IRAN

item.title:
PSILOCYBIN BEATS NICOTINE PATCHES IN QUITTING SMOKING
A study published in JAMA Network Open found a single dose of psilocybin—the active compound in psychedelic mushrooms—was far more effective than nicotine patches at helping smokers quit.
In a trial of 82 adults, over 40% of participants who received psilocybin were smoke-free after six months, compared with about 10% using nicotine patches, with both groups receiving therapy.
Lead researcher Matthew Johnson of Johns Hopkins University said the drug may help by altering brain communication and self-perception, potentially breaking addictive patterns.
The findings add momentum to growing research into psychedelic-assisted treatments, though larger trials are still needed.
item.description:
PSILOCYBIN BEATS NICOTINE PATCHES IN QUITTING SMOKING

A study published in JAMA Network Open found a single dose of psilocybin—the active compound in psychedelic mushrooms—was far more effective than nicotine patches at helping smokers quit.

In a trial of 82 adults, over 40% of participants who received psilocybin were smoke-free after six months, compared with about 10% using nicotine patches, with both groups receiving therapy.

Lead researcher Matthew Johnson of Johns Hopkins University said the drug may help by altering brain communication and self-perception, potentially breaking addictive patterns.

The findings add momentum to growing research into psychedelic-assisted treatments, though larger trials are still needed.

item.title: WITKOFF: LET'S SEE IF IRANIANS WANT TO TALK
item.description: WITKOFF: LET'S SEE IF IRANIANS WANT TO TALK

item.title:
IRAN CLAIMS WAR MOMENTUM, 90% STRIKE SUCCESS
Iran says the war is shifting in its favor. Ebrahim Rezaei, spokesman for the Iranian parliament’s security commission, claimed missile and drone strikes have a 90% success rate, though he offered no evidence.
Tehran also said it is not negotiating with the U.S., despite signals from Donald Trump that talks could be possible.
item.description:
IRAN CLAIMS WAR MOMENTUM, 90% STRIKE SUCCESS

Iran says the war is shifting in its favor. Ebrahim Rezaei, spokesman for the Iranian parliament’s security commission, claimed missile and drone strikes have a 90% success rate, though he offered no evidence.

Tehran also said it is not negotiating with the U.S., despite signals from Donald Trump that talks could be possible.

item.title:
IMPEACHMENT ODDS FOR TRUMP HIT ALL-TIME HIGH
Prediction markets are increasingly betting that Donald Trump could be impeached again before 2028.
On Kalshi, the probability has climbed to around 71% — an all-time high on the market after steadily rising from the mid-50% range late last year. https://kalshi.com/markets/kximpeach/president-impeached/kximpeach?utm_source=walterbloomberg
item.description:
IMPEACHMENT ODDS FOR TRUMP HIT ALL-TIME HIGH

Prediction markets are increasingly betting that Donald Trump could be impeached again before 2028.

On Kalshi, the probability has climbed to around 71% — an all-time high on the market after steadily rising from the mid-50% range late last year.
kalshi.com/markets/kximpeach…

item.description: The purchase is part of wider plans to prevent major artworks from being bought by private collectors.

item.title:
OPTIONS MARKET SIGNALS MORE UPSIDE FOR OIL
Even as crude prices pull back and Brent spreads ease, the options market is still positioning for higher oil.
Traders are paying the largest premium in years for call options on WTI futures relative to puts — a clear sign investors see greater risk of a price spike than a drop.
Prediction markets are also pointing higher, with year-end forecasts for WTI pushing above $120.
item.description:
OPTIONS MARKET SIGNALS MORE UPSIDE FOR OIL

Even as crude prices pull back and Brent spreads ease, the options market is still positioning for higher oil.

Traders are paying the largest premium in years for call options on WTI futures relative to puts — a clear sign investors see greater risk of a price spike than a drop.

Prediction markets are also pointing higher, with year-end forecasts for WTI pushing above $120.

item.title:
US SPENDS $5B ON MUNITIONS IN FIRST 2 DAYS OF IRAN WAR
The U.S. military used $5.6 billion in munitions in the first two days of the conflict with Iran, including long-range precision weapons and air defense rounds.
Lawmakers, including Sen. Mark Kelly, warned Congress will likely face requests for supplemental funding as stocks deplete quickly against Iran’s large missile and drone arsenal.
item.description:
US SPENDS $5B ON MUNITIONS IN FIRST 2 DAYS OF IRAN WAR

The U.S. military used $5.6 billion in munitions in the first two days of the conflict with Iran, including long-range precision weapons and air defense rounds.

Lawmakers, including Sen. Mark Kelly, warned Congress will likely face requests for supplemental funding as stocks deplete quickly against Iran’s large missile and drone arsenal.

item.title:
OIL COULD SOAR TO $150 AMID HORMUZ BLOCKADE
The US-Israel conflict with Iran has shut the Strait of Hormuz, risking the largest oil disruption in history. Experts warn prices could hit $150 per barrel by month’s end.
Amena Bakr of Kpler said halted production and ongoing strait closures outweigh Donald Trump’s reassurances about naval protection, leaving the market confidence low despite prices briefly falling below $100.
item.description:
OIL COULD SOAR TO $150 AMID HORMUZ BLOCKADE

The US-Israel conflict with Iran has shut the Strait of Hormuz, risking the largest oil disruption in history. Experts warn prices could hit $150 per barrel by month’s end.

Amena Bakr of Kpler said halted production and ongoing strait closures outweigh Donald Trump’s reassurances about naval protection, leaving the market confidence low despite prices briefly falling below $100.

item.title:
HORMUZ SHIPPING NEARLY HALTED
The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to most vessels as the Middle East conflict enters its second week.
Inbound traffic to the Persian Gulf has been at a standstill for the past 24 hours, with only Iran-linked ships continuing to pass.
item.description:
HORMUZ SHIPPING NEARLY HALTED

The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to most vessels as the Middle East conflict enters its second week.

Inbound traffic to the Persian Gulf has been at a standstill for the past 24 hours, with only Iran-linked ships continuing to pass.

item.title: Simpson and guide Poth win Paralympic silver for GB
item.description: Neil Simpson wins Great Britain's first medal of the Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics with silver in the alpine combined.

item.title: Maternity inquiry chair named as Donna Ockenden in government U-turn
item.description: Donna Ockenden is appointed to lead the maternity services inquiry at Leeds hospitals.

item.title:
CHINA’S ENERGY STRATEGY FACES IRAN WAR TEST
China has spent years preparing for a Middle East oil crisis—and the current conflict involving Iran is its biggest test yet.
To reduce vulnerability, China has built massive oil reserves—estimated at over 1.2 billion barrels, about 100 days of imports—while boosting domestic production, expanding electric vehicles, and deepening energy ties with Russia.
The strategy also limits exposure to disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil chokepoint.
So far, these measures have helped Beijing cushion the shock, though prolonged conflict could still raise costs and tighten LNG supplies for industry.
item.description:
CHINA’S ENERGY STRATEGY FACES IRAN WAR TEST

China has spent years preparing for a Middle East oil crisis—and the current conflict involving Iran is its biggest test yet.

To reduce vulnerability, China has built massive oil reserves—estimated at over 1.2 billion barrels, about 100 days of imports—while boosting domestic production, expanding electric vehicles, and deepening energy ties with Russia.

The strategy also limits exposure to disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil chokepoint.

So far, these measures have helped Beijing cushion the shock, though prolonged conflict could still raise costs and tighten LNG supplies for industry.

item.description: How one London trading floor coped with a record swing in prices

item.title:
GEN Z TURNS TO CRYPTO AND BETTING FOR WEALTH
Many Gen Z investors are turning to high-risk assets to reach financial goals, according to a study by Northwestern Mutual.
The survey found 80% of Gen Z feel financially behind and believe speculative investments can help them build wealth faster than traditional strategies.
About 32% are invested in or considering crypto, while a similar share are participating in sports betting and prediction markets.
item.description:
GEN Z TURNS TO CRYPTO AND BETTING FOR WEALTH

Many Gen Z investors are turning to high-risk assets to reach financial goals, according to a study by Northwestern Mutual.

The survey found 80% of Gen Z feel financially behind and believe speculative investments can help them build wealth faster than traditional strategies.

About 32% are invested in or considering crypto, while a similar share are participating in sports betting and prediction markets.

item.title: Glasgow Central Station still shut but seems to have avoided major damage from fire
item.description: A huge fire destroyed a neighbouring building but Network Rail staff believe the station has not been seriously damaged.
